page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance powered by the t2a family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core and no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations show average performance across most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 28983.31 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9311.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1129.87 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 129.41 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1078.0 and a weak bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 8.61 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is also limited, as indicated by a weak Passmark CPU encryption score of 2588.14, which is influenced by the lack of AVX or AMX instruction support.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-16 offers a competitive hardware profile for ARM-native workloads, delivering steady multi-threaded execution without the overhead of hyperthreading. Tradeoffs include lower single-threaded efficiency and reduced performance in vector-heavy or highly encrypted tasks. This server is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, including multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and Redis deployments.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on the 3.0 GHz ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. Lacking hyperthreading, local storage, and GPU accelerators, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28983.31 and a static web serving throughput of 18.26 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and cryptographic operations are weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1078.0 and a Passmark encryption score of 2588.14 due to the absence of AVX and AMX instructions. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for ARM-native applications, making it suitable for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-16 is an ARM-based server with 16 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 64 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: An ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring sixteen dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average multi-core performance with 28983.31 stress-ng score
Weak single-core and cryptographic performance due to lack of AVX/AMX
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suitable for static web serving, database operations, and software compilation
